concept: build the family from root ك ت ب; class before gloss
prompt: For root ك ت ب, give the verb, maṣdar, agent, patient, and place-noun, and which members are nouns.
expected_answer: Verb كَتَبَ he wrote / present يَكْتُبُ; maṣdar كِتَابَة the act of writing and كِتَاب a book; agent كَاتِب writer; patient مَكْتُوب written; place-noun مَكْتَب a desk/office. كِتَاب and مَكْتُوب are nouns and must never be glossed to write.
accepted_variants: one theme (writing), five roles; كِتَاب/مَكْتُوب are nouns
verb/maṣdar/agent/patient/place-noun of ك ت ب
required_reasoning: noun members not glossed as the verb

concept: same root, three classes: عِلْم vs عَالِم vs عَلَّمَ
prompt: For root ع ل م, distinguish عِلْم, عَالِم, and عَلَّمَ by class.
expected_answer: عِلْم = the maṣdar knowledge (noun); عَالِم = the agent a scholar/one who knows; عَلَّمَ = Form II verb he taught (shadda = causative). Same root, three classes; read the harakāt and the مـ/shadda before glossing.
accepted_variants: عِلْم noun, عَالِم agent, عَلَّمَ Form II verb
knowledge vs knower vs taught, by class
required_reasoning: maṣdar vs agent vs verb separated

concept: root أ م ن family; keep the hamza, guard against ي م ن
prompt: For root أ م ن, give the Form I and Form IV members and explain why إِيمَان is not أَيْمَان.
expected_answer: Form I أَمِنَ he was safe → maṣdar أَمْن safety; Form IV آمَنَ he believed → maṣdar إِيمَان faith (إِفْعَال); agent مُؤْمِن a believer; noun أَمَانَة a trust. The hamza is the first radical: إِيمَان (root أ م ن) is not أَيْمَان oaths (root ي م ن); norm() merges them, the hamza seat keeps them apart.
accepted_variants: root أ م ن with the hamza first radical; إِيمَان ≠ أَيْمَان
keep the hamza; the seat separates faith from oaths
forbidden_answers: إِيمَان and أَيْمَان share the same root
required_reasoning: false ي م ن merge rejected by the seat

concept: root س ل م family across forms
prompt: For root س ل م, give Form I, II, IV members and which carries the religious sense.
expected_answer: Form I سَلِمَ he was safe/whole → noun سَلَام peace; Form II سَلَّمَ he greeted/handed over; Form IV أَسْلَمَ he submitted → maṣdar إِسْلَام submission/Islam; agent مُسْلِم one who submits, fem. مُسْلِمَة. Form IV أَسْلَمَ/إِسْلَام carries the religious sense; the theme is wholeness.
accepted_variants: Form IV أَسْلَمَ/إِسْلَام = religious sense; سَلَام/سَلِمَ share wholeness
root س ل م across Forms I/II/IV
required_reasoning: religious sense bound to Form IV

concept: intensive adjectives رَحْمَٰن / رَحِيم are not the verb
prompt: For root ر ح م, classify رَحْمَٰن and رَحِيم and contrast with the verb.
expected_answer: Form I رَحِمَ he had mercy → maṣdar رَحْمَة mercy (with ة); رَحْمَٰن (فَعْلَان) and رَحِيم (فَعِيل) are two intensive adjectives of the root, glossed as the Names/attributes, not to be merciful; noun رَحِم womb shares the root.
accepted_variants: رَحْمَٰن/رَحِيم = intensive adjectives, not the verb
gloss them as attributes, not to be merciful
forbidden_answers: gloss رَحِيم as to be merciful
all members are the same class

concept: hollow root ق و ل; the weak middle radical
prompt: For root ق و ل, give the verb, maṣdar, and agent, and explain the alif in قَالَ.
expected_answer: Form I قَالَ he said (the و surfaces as ا) / present يَقُولُ he says (و returns); maṣdar قَوْل a saying/word (noun); agent قَائِل a sayer/speaker. The middle و is the weak radical; the alif in قَالَ is it, not a stray long vowel.
accepted_variants: root ق و ل hollow; alif = the weak و radical
قَالَ/يَقُولُ/قَوْل/قَائِل with the و returning
forbidden_answers: the alif is a stray vowel

concept: two singulars split by harakāt; bind each plural to its singular
prompt: For root ر ج ل, give the two singulars and their plurals, split by the harakāt.
expected_answer: رَجُل a man (fatḥa-ḍamma) → broken plural رِجَال men (فِعَال); رِجْل a foot/leg (kasra-sukūn) → plural أَرْجُل feet (أَفْعَال). Two singulars share the consonants, split by the harakāt; bind each plural to the singular it pluralizes and never gloss رِجَال as feet.
accepted_variants: رَجُل→رِجَال men, رِجْل→أَرْجُل feet; split by harakāt
two singulars by vowels; bind plurals correctly
required_reasoning: each plural bound to its singular
sense not crossed between the two

concept: root ن ص ر family: act, agent(s), patient
prompt: For root ن ص ر, give the maṣdar, agent, plural, and patient and their roles.
expected_answer: Form I نَصَرَ he helped → maṣdar نَصْر help/victory (noun); agent نَاصِر a helper, plural أَنْصَار helpers/supporters; patient مَنْصُور (one) helped / given victory. Three roles, one root: the act, the helper(s), the helped one.
accepted_variants: نَصْر act, نَاصِر/أَنْصَار helper(s), مَنْصُور helped one
one root, three roles

prompt: For root ك ف ر, distinguish كُفْر and كَافِر by class.
expected_answer: Form I كَفَرَ he disbelieved/covered → maṣdar كُفْر disbelief (noun); agent كَافِر a disbeliever; broken plural كُفَّار (فُعَّال) / sound plural كَافِرُونَ. كُفْر is the noun disbelief; كَافِر is the agent disbeliever: same root, different class, different gloss vocabulary. Class first.
accepted_variants: كُفْر noun disbelief, كَافِر agent disbeliever
class first, then gloss
forbidden_answers: كُفْر and كَافِر are the same

concept: Form I vs Form IV flips doing to causing
prompt: For root د خ ل, contrast Form I and Form IV and the sense shift.
expected_answer: Form I دَخَلَ he entered / present يَدْخُلُ; maṣdar دُخُول entering/entry (noun); Form IV أَدْخَلَ he caused to enter/admitted (causative) → patient/place مُدْخَل place or act of entering. The form flips the sense from doing to causing; the root theme is constant.
accepted_variants: Form I enter vs Form IV cause-to-enter; root constant
the prefix أ flips to the causative sense
forbidden_answers: Form I and Form IV mean the same

concept: proper-name vs common-word collision is a context question
prompt: When a surface could be both a proper name and a common lexical item of the same root, what decides, and what is concept metadata worth?
expected_answer: The verse decides. A proper-name/common-word collision is a context question, not a concept-map answer; concept metadata is only a review flag. Do not let a host-only family gloss swallow بـ, لـ, كـ, وَ, فَـ, or an object suffix, and strip visible proclitics and suffix pronouns before mapping to a root family.
accepted_variants: the verse decides; concept metadata is only a review flag
name vs common is contextual, not from the concept map
forbidden_answers: the concept map decides name vs common
always treat it as a proper name
let the family gloss swallow the proclitics
required_reasoning: collision treated as a context question
concept metadata downgraded to a review flag
visible clitics handled before the family gloss
